FrameworkSpecIterations.Subject: fix(code-builder): explicit emission directives for GetRecord + FetchChanges per metadata capability
FrameworkSpecIterations.Reason: Validator I7 walks the concrete class via ts-morph; inheritance is invisible. Spec didn't say so explicitly.

FrameworkSpecIterations.Subject: fix(code-builder): nail down FetchChanges watermark-seeding rules (3 edge cases)
FrameworkSpecIterations.Reason: Two §13.4 unit tests caught real bugs in agent's first emission — malformed watermark dominating max-comparison; non-incremental IO surfacing prior watermark. Spec phrasing of max-watermark line was ambiguous.

InvariantResults.WarningRoot: Invariant3_FKMetadataCorrectness — RelatedIntegrationObjectFieldName null on 10 FKs where target IO lacks detectable PK in OpenAPI source (source-level gap, not extractor bug)

Measurements.PeakHeapAbsoluteNote: Informational only — dominated by vitest base + loaded modules + 1.7MB hubspot.json; the connector's own cost is the 0.6MB delta. Absolute is not asserted against the 500MB ceiling because it would measure the test harness, not the connector.
Measurements.ConcurrentFetchIsolation: Verified — 3-IO Promise.all returns correctly-isolated per-IO responses

CoverageMatrix_Tally.NA_Row: Row 16 (hierarchy detection) — HubSpot's static OpenAPI uses flat resource paths; no parent-child URL templates exist in the source. 0 IOs with ParentObjectName populated, accurate to source.
CoverageMatrix_Tally.Skipped_Row: Row 36 (T10 live API) — scaffold exists at HubSpotConnector.live.test.ts; describe.skipIf(!HUBSPOT_LIVE_TESTING) gate skips cleanly without credentials. Per credential-guard rule, agent never reads credential bytes; MCP subprocess is the authorized path. MCP runner does not yet have a T10 tier vocabulary.

Incremental_5_Scenarios_§13_4.1_FirstSync: Pass — no watermark, no modifiedSince param, NewWatermarkValue = max(updatedAt) across batch
Incremental_5_Scenarios_§13_4.2_SubsequentSync: Pass — ctx.WatermarkValue present, modifiedSince URL-encoded into query, NewWatermarkValue ≥ incoming
Incremental_5_Scenarios_§13_4.3_OutOfOrderBatch: Pass — records [t3, t1, t2] yield NewWatermarkValue = t3 (max-seen, NOT most-recent-in-batch)
Incremental_5_Scenarios_§13_4.4_PartialFailure: Pass — HTTP 500 throws, engine catches, watermark untouched
Incremental_5_Scenarios_§13_4.5_FormatMismatch: Pass — malformed 'this-is-not-a-timestamp' → no modifiedSince added, baseline reset, NewWatermarkValue derives from batch alone

Validator: consider 'AcceptableFollowUp' status for source-level gaps that warning currently surfaces

Deferred: metadata/actions/integrations-auto-generated/.hubspot-actions.json — generator is DB-read-driven (requires mj-cli auth context with bound CurrentUser; bare tsx invocation fails with TypeError). Even with auth, current DB state is pre-Phase-B-expansion so generator output would match the existing 39-action file. Two unblocks: (a) refactor generator to add metadata-file-driven mode; (b) defer to a post-B.4 batch event with one mj-sync push of all 4 vendors' metadata + a single regenerator pass. Per user (this run): path (b).

DecisionPointsForB2_Salesforce: Base-class lifting of FetchChanges: deferred per '≥3 vendors required' rule
Invariant 7 walking class hierarchy: deferred alongside base-class refactor
Salesforce will likely surface SOQL-filter + ETag-concurrency edge cases that further tighten the CodeBuilder spec
Salesforce uses sObject describe API for runtime schema (richer than HubSpot's custom-properties API) — Phase-B custom-object handling may need different shape per vendor

InformationalRetraction.Verdict: Not grounded — sloppy session-prose with no corresponding code change. Searches of .claude/agents/ and .claude/rules/ confirm zero ServiceStack references. Treating as retracted: no ServiceStack-aware role file exists or was promised.
